([/ˈæntɪˌpoʊd, ænˈtɪpədi/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")) of any spot on [Earth](/wiki/Earth "Earth") is the point on Earth's surface [diametrically](/wiki/Diameter "Diameter") opposite to it. A pair of points *antipodal* ([/ænˈtɪpədəl/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")) to each other are situated such that a [straight line](/wiki/Line_(geometry) "Line (geometry)") connecting the two would pass through Earth's center. [Antipodal points](/wiki/Antipodal_point "Antipodal point") are as far away from each other as possible.[[note 1]](#cite_note-2) The [North](/wiki/North_Pole "North Pole") and [South Poles](/wiki/South_Pole "South Pole") are antipodes of each other.

to their inhabitants.[[2]](#cite_note-3) Geographically, the antipodes of the British Isles are in the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean"), south of New Zealand. This gave rise to the name of the [Antipodes Islands](/wiki/Antipodes_Islands "Antipodes Islands") of New Zealand, which are close to the antipode of [London](/wiki/London "London"). The antipodes of [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") are in the [North Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"), while parts of [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), [France](/wiki/France "France") and [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco") are antipodal to New Zealand.

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/64/Antipodes_LAEA.png/250px-Antipodes_LAEA.png)](/wiki/File:Antipodes_LAEA.png)

This map shows the antipode of each point on [Earth](/wiki/Earth "Earth")'s surface—the points where the blue and yellow overlap are land antipodes; most land has its antipodes in the ocean. This map uses the [Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection](/wiki/Lambert_azimuthal_equal-area_projection "Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection"). The yellow areas are the reflections through Earth's center of land masses of the opposite [Western Hemisphere](/wiki/Western_Hemisphere "Western Hemisphere").

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/36/Antipodes_LAEA_inverted.png/250px-Antipodes_LAEA_inverted.png)](/wiki/File:Antipodes_LAEA_inverted.png)

The same map, from the perspective of the Western Hemisphere. Here the blue areas are the reflections of the [Eastern Hemisphere](/wiki/Eastern_Hemisphere "Eastern Hemisphere").

In [geography](/wiki/Geography "Geography"), the **antipode** ([/ˈæntɪˌpoʊd, ænˈtɪpədi/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")) of any spot on [Earth](/wiki/Earth "Earth") is the point on Earth's surface [diametrically](/wiki/Diameter "Diameter") opposite to it. A pair of points *antipodal* ([/ænˈtɪpədəl/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English")) to each other are situated such that a [straight line](/wiki/Line_(geometry) "Line (geometry)") connecting the two would pass through Earth's center. [Antipodal points](/wiki/Antipodal_point "Antipodal point") are as far away from each other as possible.[[note 1]](#cite_note-2) The [North](/wiki/North_Pole "North Pole") and [South Poles](/wiki/South_Pole "South Pole") are antipodes of each other.

In the [Northern Hemisphere](/wiki/Northern_Hemisphere "Northern Hemisphere"), "the Antipodes" may refer to [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"), and **Antipodeans** to their inhabitants.[[2]](#cite_note-3) Geographically, the antipodes of the British Isles are in the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean"), south of New Zealand. This gave rise to the name of the [Antipodes Islands](/wiki/Antipodes_Islands "Antipodes Islands") of New Zealand, which are close to the antipode of [London](/wiki/London "London"). The antipodes of [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") are in the [North Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"), while parts of [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain"), [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), [France](/wiki/France "France") and [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco") are antipodal to New Zealand.

Approximately 15% of land territory is antipodal to other land, representing approximately 4.4% of Earth's surface.[[3]](#cite_note-Sawe2017-4) Another source estimates that about 3% of Earth's surface is antipodal land.[[4]](#cite_note-Spilhaus1991-5) The largest antipodal land masses are the [Malay Archipelago](/wiki/Malay_Archipelago "Malay Archipelago"), antipodal to the [Amazon basin](/wiki/Amazon_basin "Amazon basin") and adjoining [Andean](/wiki/Andes "Andes") ranges; east [China](/wiki/China "China") and [Mongolia](/wiki/Mongolia "Mongolia"), and small sections of southeast [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia"), antipodal to [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina") and [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile"); and [Greenland](/wiki/Greenland "Greenland") and the [Canadian Arctic Archipelago](/wiki/Arctic_Archipelago "Arctic Archipelago"), antipodal to [East Antarctica](/wiki/East_Antarctica "East Antarctica"). There is a general paucity of antipodal land because the Southern Hemisphere has comparatively less land than the Northern Hemisphere and, of that, the antipodes of Australia are in the [North Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"), while the antipodes of southern [Africa](/wiki/Africa "Africa") are in the [North Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean").

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Geography")]

Since the antipode of any place on the Earth is the place that is diametrically opposite of it, a line drawn from one to the other will pass through the centre of Earth and form a true [diameter](/wiki/Diameter "Diameter").[[5]](#cite_note-EB1911-6) For example, the antipodes of most of New Zealand's [North Island](/wiki/North_Island "North Island") lie in Spain. Most of the Earth's land surfaces have ocean at their antipodes; this is a natural consequence of most of the Earth's surface being covered in water.

The antipode of any place on Earth is distant from it by 180° of [longitude](/wiki/Longitude "Longitude") and as many degrees to the north of the [Equator](/wiki/Equator "Equator") as the original is to the south (or vice versa); in other words, the [latitudes](/wiki/Latitude "Latitude") are numerically equal, but one is north and the other south.[[5]](#cite_note-EB1911-6) The maps shown here are based on this relationship; they show a [Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection](/wiki/Lambert_azimuthal_equal-area_projection "Lambert azimuthal equal-area projection") of the Earth, in yellow, overlaid on which is another map, in blue, shifted horizontally by 180° of longitude and inverted about the Equator with respect to latitude.

[Solar noon](/wiki/Solar_noon "Solar noon") (i.e. the time at which the sun is highest) at one place is [solar midnight](/wiki/Solar_midnight "Solar midnight") at the other, the [winter solstice](/wiki/Winter_solstice "Winter solstice") at one place is the [summer solstice](/wiki/Summer_solstice "Summer solstice") at the other, and the [spring equinox](/wiki/Spring_equinox_(disambiguation) "Spring equinox (disambiguation)") at one place is the [fall equinox](/wiki/Autumnal_equinox_(disambiguation) "Autumnal equinox (disambiguation)") at the other. Sunrise and sunset do not quite oppose each other at antipodes due to refraction of sunlight.

### Mathematical description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Mathematical description")]

If the [geographic coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate "Geographic coordinate") ([latitude](/wiki/Latitude "Latitude") and [longitude](/wiki/Longitude "Longitude")) of a point on the Earth's surface are (*φ*, *θ*), then the coordinates of the antipodal point are (−*φ*, *θ* ± 180°). This relation holds true whether the Earth is approximated as a perfect [sphere](/wiki/Sphere "Sphere") or as a [reference ellipsoid](/wiki/Reference_ellipsoid "Reference ellipsoid").

In terms of the usual way these geographic coordinates are given, this [transformation](/wiki/Transformation_(function) "Transformation (function)") can be expressed symbolically as

:   *x*° N/S *y*° E/W  ↦  *x*° S/N (180 − *y*)° W/E,

that is, for the latitude (the north–south coordinate) the magnitude of the angle remains the same but N is changed to S and vice versa, and for the longitude (the East/West coordinate) the angle is replaced by its [supplementary angle](/wiki/Supplementary_angle "Supplementary angle") while E is exchanged for W. For example, the antipode of the point in China at 37° N 119° E (a few hundred kilometres from [Beijing](/wiki/Beijing "Beijing")) is the point in Argentina at 37° S 61° W (a few hundred kilometres from [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Buenos_Aires "Buenos Aires")).

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Etymology")]

The word *antipodes* comes from the [Greek:](/wiki/Ancient_Greek "Ancient Greek") ἀντίποδες (antípodes),[[6]](#cite_note-7) plural of ἀντίπους (antipous), "with feet opposite (ours)",[[7]](#cite_note-8) from ἀντί (antí, "opposite") + πούς ([poús](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%CF%80%CE%BF%CF%8D%CF%82 "wikt:πούς"), "foot"). The Greek word is attested in [Plato](/wiki/Plato "Plato")'s dialogue *[Timaeus](/wiki/Timaeus_(dialogue) "Timaeus (dialogue)")*, already referring to a spherical Earth, explaining the relativity of the terms "above" and "below":

> For if there were any solid body in equipoise at the centre of the universe, there would be nothing to draw it to this extreme rather than to that, for they are all perfectly similar; and if a person were to go round the world in a circle, he would often, when standing at the antipodes of his former position, speak of the same point as above and below; for, as I was saying just now, to speak of the whole which is in the form of a globe as having one part above and another below is not like a sensible man.

— Plato[[8]](#cite_note-9)

The term is taken up by [Aristotle](/wiki/Aristotle "Aristotle") (*De caelo* 308a.20), [Strabo](/wiki/Strabo "Strabo") (*[Geographica](/wiki/Geographica "Geographica")* 1.1.13), [Plutarch](/wiki/Plutarch "Plutarch") (*[On the Malice of Herodotus](/wiki/On_the_Malice_of_Herodotus "On the Malice of Herodotus")* 37) and [Diogenes Laërtius](/wiki/Diogenes_La%C3%ABrtius "Diogenes Laërtius") (*[Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers](/wiki/Lives_and_Opinions_of_Eminent_Philosophers "Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers")* book 3), and was adopted into [Latin](/wiki/Latin "Latin") as *antipodes*. The Latin word changed its sense from the original "under the feet, opposite side" to "those with the feet opposite", i.e. a [bahuvrihi](/wiki/Bahuvrihi "Bahuvrihi") referring to hypothetical people living on the opposite side of the Earth. Medieval illustrations imagine them in some way "inverted", with their feet growing out of their heads, pointing upward.

In this sense, *Antipodes* first entered [English](/wiki/English_language "English language") in 1398 in a translation of the 13th century *De Proprietatibus Rerum* by [Bartholomeus Anglicus](/wiki/Bartholomeus_Anglicus "Bartholomeus Anglicus"), translated by [John of Trevisa](/wiki/John_of_Trevisa "John of Trevisa"):

> Yonde in Ethiopia ben the Antipodes, men that haue theyr fete ayenst our fete.

(*In Modern English: Yonder in Ethiopia are the Antipodes, men that have their feet against our feet.*)

The modern English singular *antipode* arose in the 16th or 17th century as a [back-formation](/wiki/Back-formation "Back-formation") from *antipodes*; *antipous* or the Latinate *antipus* would have been closer to the original singular. Most dictionaries suggest a pronunciation of [/ˈæntɪˌpoʊd/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") for this form.[[9]](#cite_note-10)[[10]](#cite_note-11)[[11]](#cite_note-12)

## Historical significance

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Historical significance")]

[Pomponius Mela](/wiki/Pomponius_Mela "Pomponius Mela"), the first Roman geographer, asserted that the Earth had two habitable zones, a North and South one, but that it would be impossible to get into contact with each other because of [the unbearable heat at the Equator](/wiki/Torrid_zone "Torrid zone") (*De orbis situ* 1.4).[[note 2]](#cite_note-14)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/14/Crates_Terrestrial_Sphere.png/250px-Crates_Terrestrial_Sphere.png)](/wiki/File:Crates_Terrestrial_Sphere.png)

The Terrestrial Sphere of [Crates of Mallus](/wiki/Crates_of_Mallus "Crates of Mallus") (c. 150 BCE), showing the region of the antipodes in the southern half of the western hemisphere

Third-century AD Christian philosopher [Augustine of Hippo](/wiki/Augustine_of_Hippo "Augustine of Hippo") was skeptical of the notion. Augustine asserted that "it is too absurd to say that some men might have set sail from this side and, traversing the immense expanse of ocean, have propagated there a race of human beings descended from that one first man."[[13]](#cite_note-15)

In the [Early Middle Ages](/wiki/Early_Middle_Ages "Early Middle Ages"), [Isidore of Seville](/wiki/Isidore_of_Seville "Isidore of Seville")'s widely read [encyclopedia](/wiki/Etymologiae "Etymologiae") presented the term "antipodes" or, as he said "antipodas" as referring to [antichthones](/wiki/Antichthones "Antichthones") (people who lived on the opposite side of the Earth), as well as to a geographical place:

> Apart from these three parts of the world, there exists a fourth part beyond the interior Ocean; it is in the south and is unknown to us because of the burning heat of the Sun; within its borders the fabled Antipodeans are reputed to dwell.[[14]](#cite_note-16)

In using the form *antipodas* rather than the more usual Latin *antipodes* Isidore simply transcribed the original Greek αντίποδας, the singular case of the name: the plural case is αντίποδες (antipodes), used in converting the name into Latin. These people came to play a role in medieval discussions about the [shape of the Earth](/wiki/Flat_Earth#Europe:_Early_Middle_Ages "Flat Earth").[[15]](#cite_note-17)

In 748, in reply to a letter from [Boniface](/wiki/Saint_Boniface "Saint Boniface"), [Pope Zachary](/wiki/Pope_Zachary "Pope Zachary") declared the belief "that beneath the earth there was another world and other men, another sun and moon" to be heretical. In his letter, Boniface had apparently maintained that [Vergilius of Salzburg](/wiki/Vergilius_of_Salzburg "Vergilius of Salzburg") held such a belief.[[16]](#cite_note-18)
[[17]](#cite_note-19)
[[18]](#cite_note-20)

The antipodes being an attribute of a [spherical Earth](/wiki/Spherical_Earth "Spherical Earth"), some ancient authors used their perceived absurdity as an argument for a [flat Earth](/wiki/Flat_Earth "Flat Earth").[[19]](#cite_note-21) However, knowledge of the spherical Earth was widespread during the Middle Ages, only occasionally disputed—the medieval dispute surrounding the antipodes mainly concerned the question whether people could live on the opposite side of the Earth: since the torrid [clime](/wiki/Clime "Clime") was considered impassable, it would have been impossible to [evangelize](/wiki/Evangelize "Evangelize") them. This posed the problem that [Christ](/wiki/Christ "Christ") told the apostles to evangelize all mankind; with regard to the unreachable antipodes, this would have been impossible. Christ would either have appeared a second time, in the antipodes, or left the damned irredeemable. Such an argument was forwarded by the Spanish theologian [Alonso Tostado](/wiki/Alonso_Tostado "Alonso Tostado") as late as the 15th century and "St. Augustine doubts" was a response to Columbus's proposal to sail westwards to the Indies.[[20]](#cite_note-22)

The author of the Norwegian book *[Konungs Skuggsjá](/wiki/Konungs_Skuggsj%C3%A1 "Konungs Skuggsjá")*, from around 1250, discusses the existence of antipodes. He notes that (if they exist) they will see the sun in the north in the middle of the day and that they will have seasons opposite those of the Northern Hemisphere.

[Herodotus](/wiki/Herodotus "Herodotus") recorded that Pharaoh [Necho II](/wiki/Necho_II "Necho II") of the 26th Dynasty (610–595 BC) commissioned an expedition of Phoenicians which in three years sailed from the Red Sea around Africa back to the mouth of the Nile, and that "*as they sailed on a westerly course round the southern end of Libya (Africa), they had the sun on their right*"— to northward of them, proving that they had been in the Southern Hemisphere.[[21]](#cite_note-23) The earliest surviving account by a European who had visited the Southern Hemisphere is that of [Marco Polo](/wiki/Marco_Polo "Marco Polo") (who, on his way home in 1292, sailed south of the [Malay Peninsula](/wiki/Malay_Peninsula "Malay Peninsula")). He noted that it was impossible to see the star [Polaris](/wiki/Polaris "Polaris") from there.

[![Six men with spears walking around a circle. An Old French text says "hőme qui va en tour le monde".](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/42/La_Terre_est_ronde%2C_Image_du_Monde%2C_Gossuin_de_Metz.png/250px-La_Terre_est_ronde%2C_Image_du_Monde%2C_Gossuin_de_Metz.png)](/wiki/File:La_Terre_est_ronde,_Image_du_Monde,_Gossuin_de_Metz.png)

In this illustration of a thought experiment, two men walk in opposite directions and meet at the antipodes. 14th-century *Image du monde*.

The idea of dry land in the southern climes, the *[Terra Australis](/wiki/Terra_Australis "Terra Australis")*, was introduced by [Ptolemy](/wiki/Ptolemy "Ptolemy") and appears on European maps as an imaginary continent from the 15th century. *Antipodes* was what [Giovanni Contarini](/wiki/Giovanni_Matteo_Contarini "Giovanni Matteo Contarini"), on his world map of 1506 called the land later named *America* by [Martin Waldseemüller](/wiki/Martin_Waldseem%C3%BCller "Martin Waldseemüller").[[22]](#cite_note-24) When the land discovered by [Pedro Alvarez Cabral](/wiki/Pedro_Alvarez_Cabral "Pedro Alvarez Cabral") in April 1500, [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), was formally named *Santa Cruz* by the assembled Portuguese court on 20 May 1503, it was also referred to in the official record of the proceedings as the "Land of the Antipodes": *terra Antipodum*.[[23]](#cite_note-25)

The land reached by Columbus in 1492 was identified as that of the Antipodes by the diplomatist Peter Martyr who, in a letter he wrote from Barcelona dated 14 May 1493, said: "A few days since, a certain Christopher Columbus, a Ligurian, returned from the Western Antipodes".[[24]](#cite_note-26) Perhaps influenced by this, Fernão Vaz Dourado in his Atlas of 1571 inscribed over the map of Mexico and adjacent parts of America, *Tera Antipodum regis Castelle inventa a Xforo Columbo Januensi* (Land of the Antipodes, discovered for the King of Castile by Christopher Columbus of Genoa).[[25]](#cite_note-27)

In spite of having been discovered relatively late by European explorers, [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") was inhabited very early in human history; the ancestors of the [Indigenous Australians](/wiki/Indigenous_Australians "Indigenous Australians") reached it at least 50,000 years ago.

## True trip "around the world"

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: True trip \"around the world\"")]

To make the longest distance trip around the planet, a traveler would have to pass through a set of antipodal points. *All meridians* can be crossed in one hemisphere—indeed, this is possible by walking in a circle around one of the poles—but such trips are shorter than a minimum circumnavigation. On the other hand, the greatest straight line distance that could in theory be covered is a trip exactly on the Equator, a distance of 40,075 kilometres (24,901 mi). The Earth's [equatorial bulge](/wiki/Equatorial_bulge "Equatorial bulge") makes this slightly longer than a north–south trip around the world along a set of meridian lines, which is a distance of 40,008 kilometres (24,860 mi). Any other closed [great circle](/wiki/Great_circle "Great circle") route starting on the equator and traveling at an angle between 0° (an equatorial route) and 90° (a polar route) would be between 40,075 and 40,008 kilometres (24,901 and 24,860 mi). In all of these cases, after half of the world has been traversed, every subsequent point will be antipodal to one already visited.

## Air travel between antipodes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Air travel between antipodes")]

### Non-stop antipodal flights by commercial aircraft (scheduled)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Non-stop antipodal flights by commercial aircraft (scheduled)")]

There are currently no commercial aircraft capable of traveling non-stop between antipodes with a standard full commercial passenger load.

The current world record-holder [Airbus A350-900ULR](/wiki/Airbus_A350_XWB#A350-900ULR "Airbus A350 XWB") is capable of flying 18,000 kilometres (9,700 nmi; 11,000 mi),[[26]](#cite_note-28) or roughly 90% of an average antipodal distance. [Singapore Airlines](/wiki/Singapore_Airlines "Singapore Airlines") currently holds the world record for the [longest scheduled passenger flight](/wiki/Longest_flights "Longest flights"), and utilizes this model in their non-stop Singapore to New York-JFK route SQ23/24.[[27]](#cite_note-29)

In 2019, [Qantas](/wiki/Qantas "Qantas") completed separate non-stop flights taking 19–20 hours to encompass the 16,013 km (9950 miles) from New York and 17,016 km (10,573 miles) from London, both to Sydney, Australia with a limit of 49 passengers on the [Boeing 787 Dreamliner](/wiki/Boeing_787_Dreamliner "Boeing 787 Dreamliner") and who underwent medical tests on the flight. The [London-Sydney](/wiki/Kangaroo_Route "Kangaroo Route") direct routes are said to be the world's most profitable [ultra-long haul](/wiki/Ultra_long-haul "Ultra long-haul") flights annually. Their plans for the same pair of experiments were quickly put on hold due to global travel restrictions throughout the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[28]](#cite_note-30)[[29]](#cite_note-31)

### Non-stop antipodal flights by commercial aircraft (chartered)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Non-stop antipodal flights by commercial aircraft (chartered)")]

In March 2021, a [Comlux](/wiki/Comlux "Comlux") [787-8](/wiki/Boeing_787_Dreamliner "Boeing 787 Dreamliner"), registered P4-787, flew a non-scheduled (chartered), non-stop flight from [Seoul Incheon](/wiki/Incheon_International_Airport "Incheon International Airport") to [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Ministro_Pistarini_International_Airport "Ministro Pistarini International Airport"), which are nearly antipodal points. This set a new record for the longest commercial non-stop flight with paying passengers, covering 19,483 kilometres (10,520 nmi; 12,106 mi) in 20 hours 19 minutes.

The business jet variant of the [Airbus A350](/wiki/Airbus_A350 "Airbus A350"), the [ACJ350](/wiki/Airbus_A350#ACJ350 "Airbus A350"), which entered into service in 2020, has a range of 20,550 km (12,770 miles),[[30]](#cite_note-32) enabling it to operate between any two available antipodes. As of September 2021,[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit) there are three ACJ350s now in service globally. The owner of the first ACJ350, the German Government, has already taken it on a close to antipodal flight with a flight from [Cologne, Germany](/wiki/Cologne,_Germany "Cologne, Germany") to [Canberra, Australia](/wiki/Canberra,_Australia "Canberra, Australia") in November 2020. The upcoming Boeing business jet variant, the [BBJ 777-8](/wiki/Boeing_777X#BBJ_777X "Boeing 777X"), will also have an antipodal reach with its published range of 21,570 km (13,403 miles).[[31]](#cite_note-33) Both aforementioned variants from Airbus and Boeing are the first aircraft designed to handle flights exceeding the Earth's average antipodal distance of 20,000 km (12,420 miles).

### Direct antipodal flights (scheduled)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Direct antipodal flights (scheduled)")]

Among flights with fuel stop and crew-change stop but still same flight number, [Air New Zealand](/wiki/Air_New_Zealand "Air New Zealand") previously had the world's longest active scheduled flight — the [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland")–[Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles")–[London](/wiki/London "London") marathon, with an 'Origin - Destination great-circle distance' of 18,360 km or 11,410 mi and an 'Origin - Stop - Destination great-circle distance' of 19,240 km (11,960 mi) — until the airline cancelled this route in late 2019.

[The current record holder](/wiki/Longest_flights#Direct_flights_with_stops "Longest flights") is the even longer [China Eastern Airlines](/wiki/China_Eastern_Airlines "China Eastern Airlines") flight MU745 from [Shanghai](/wiki/Shanghai "Shanghai") - [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland") - [Buenos Aires](/wiki/Buenos_Aires "Buenos Aires").[[32]](#cite_note-34) This flight is by far the longest scheduled passenger flight in history and the first near-antipodal scheduled flight, being only approximately 443 km shorter than an exact antipode. It has an 'Origin - Destination great-circle distance' of 19,595 kilometres (12,176 mi; 10,580 nmi)[[33]](#cite_note-35) and an 'Origin - Stop - Destination great-circle distance' of 19,680 kilometres (12,229 mi; 10,626 nmi).[[34]](#cite_note-36)

### Future theoretical antipodal routes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Future theoretical antipodal routes")]

A hypothetically almost perfect antipodal flight would be [Tangier Ibn Battouta Airport](/wiki/Tangier_Ibn_Battouta_Airport "Tangier Ibn Battouta Airport"), [Morocco](/wiki/Morocco "Morocco") ([IATA](/wiki/IATA "IATA"): TNG), to [Whangarei Aerodrome](/wiki/Whangarei_Aerodrome "Whangarei Aerodrome"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") (IATA: WRE), whose designated locators are 10,800 nautical miles (12,428 mi; 20,002 km) apart,[[35]](#cite_note-37) almost the maximum possible distance. However, with only a length of 3,599 ft (1,097 m), Whangarei's runway is too short to accommodate any current (as of 2025[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit)) commercial jet airliner, especially one with the required range. Traveling between them would currently need at least two plane changes.

Other near-antipodal major city pairs include:[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

* [Taipei](/wiki/Taipei "Taipei") and [Asunción](/wiki/Asunci%C3%B3n "Asunción"): 19,912 km (12,373 mi) apart
* [Santiago](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago") and [Xi'an](/wiki/Xi%27an "Xi'an"): 19,897 km (12,363 mi) apart
* [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid") and [Wellington](/wiki/Wellington "Wellington"): 19,876 km (12,350 mi) apart
* [Tangier](/wiki/Tangier "Tangier") and [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland"): 19,859 km (12,340 mi) apart
* [Jakarta](/wiki/Jakarta "Jakarta") and [Bogotá](/wiki/Bogot%C3%A1 "Bogotá"): 19,808 km (12,308 mi) apart
* [Quito](/wiki/Quito "Quito") and [Kuala Lumpur](/wiki/Kuala_Lumpur "Kuala Lumpur"): 19,700 km (12,200 mi) apart
* [Hanoi](/wiki/Hanoi "Hanoi") and [La Paz](/wiki/La_Paz "La Paz"): 19,210 km (11,940 mi) apart
* [Johannesburg](/wiki/Johannesburg "Johannesburg") and [Honolulu](/wiki/Honolulu "Honolulu"): 19,188 km (11,923 mi) apart

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cf/Antipodes_equirectangular.svg/960px-Antipodes_equirectangular.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Antipodes_equirectangular.svg)

Some cities and towns which are near-antipodes in [equirectangular projection](/wiki/Equirectangular_projection "Equirectangular projection"). **Blue** labels pertain to cyan and **brown** labels pertain to yellow areas. Areas where cyan and yellow overlap (coloured green) are land antipodes.

Around 71% of the Earth's surface is covered by the [ocean](/wiki/Ocean "Ocean"), and seven-eighths of the Earth's land (when excluding Antarctica) is confined to the [land hemisphere](/wiki/Land_and_water_hemispheres "Land and water hemispheres"), so the majority of locations on land do not have land-based antipodes. About 15% of the Earth's land has an antipode on land.[[3]](#cite_note-Sawe2017-4) Rough calculation shows that, of the 29% of the earth that is covered by land, if 15% of that has antipodes on land, then about 4% (0.15 × 29% = 4.35%) of the earth's surface has antipodes that are both land surfaces. [Spilhaus](/wiki/Athelstan_Spilhaus "Athelstan Spilhaus") estimates this at about 3%.[[4]](#cite_note-Spilhaus1991-5)

The two largest human-inhabited antipodal areas are located in East Asia (mainly eastern China) and South America (mainly Argentina and Chile). The two largest monolithic antipodal land areas are most of Chile and Argentina along with eastern and central China and Mongolia, and most of Greenland along with a part of Antarctica. The Australian mainland is the largest landmass with its antipodes entirely in ocean, although some locations of mainland Australia and Tasmania are close to being antipodes of islands (Bermuda, Azores, Puerto Rico) in the North Atlantic Ocean. The largest landmass with antipodes entirely on land is the island of [Borneo](/wiki/Borneo "Borneo"), whose antipodes are in the Amazon rainforest.

#### Cities and geographic features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Cities and geographic features")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f9/Antipodes%2C_Alzon%2C_Gard%2C_France%2C_Sept._2008.jpg/250px-Antipodes%2C_Alzon%2C_Gard%2C_France%2C_Sept._2008.jpg)](/wiki/File:Antipodes,_Alzon,_Gard,_France,_Sept._2008.jpg)

The villages of [Alzon](/wiki/Alzon "Alzon"), [France](/wiki/France "France") and [Waitangi](/wiki/Waitangi,_Chatham_Islands "Waitangi, Chatham Islands"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") are an example of antipodal settlements.

##### North and South Poles

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: North and South Poles")]

By definition, the [North Pole](/wiki/North_Pole "North Pole") and the [South Pole](/wiki/South_Pole "South Pole") are antipodes.

##### Gibraltar and New Zealand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Gibraltar and New Zealand")]

[Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar "Gibraltar") is approximately antipodal to [Te Arai](/wiki/Te_Arai "Te Arai") - about 85 km (53 mi) north of [Auckland](/wiki/Auckland "Auckland"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"). This illustrates the old yet correct saying that the [sun never sets](/wiki/The_empire_on_which_the_sun_never_sets "The empire on which the sun never sets") on the [British Empire](/wiki/British_Empire "British Empire"); the sun still does not set on the [Commonwealth of Nations](/wiki/Commonwealth_of_Nations "Commonwealth of Nations").

##### New Caledonia and Mauritania

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: New Caledonia and Mauritania")]

The northern part of [New Caledonia](/wiki/New_Caledonia "New Caledonia"), an overseas territory of France, is antipodal to some thinly populated desert in [Mauritania](/wiki/Mauritania "Mauritania"), a part of the former [French West Africa](/wiki/French_West_Africa "French West Africa").

##### Suriname and Indonesia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Suriname and Indonesia")]

Portions of [Suriname](/wiki/Suriname "Suriname"), a former Dutch colony, are antipodal to [Sulawesi](/wiki/Sulawesi "Sulawesi"), an [Indonesian](/wiki/Indonesia "Indonesia") island spelled *Celebes* when it was part of the [Netherlands East Indies](/wiki/Netherlands_East_Indies "Netherlands East Indies").

##### Philippines and Bolivia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Philippines and Bolivia")]

[Luzon](/wiki/Luzon "Luzon"), the largest island of the [Philippines](/wiki/Philippines "Philippines"), is antipodal to eastern [Bolivia](/wiki/Bolivia "Bolivia"). Both countries were once part of the [Spanish Empire](/wiki/Spanish_Empire "Spanish Empire").

##### Brazil and South Korea

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Brazil and South Korea")]

[Santa Vitória do Palmar](/wiki/Santa_Vit%C3%B3ria_do_Palmar "Santa Vitória do Palmar"), the most southerly town of more than 10,000 people in [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), is antipodal to [Jeju Island](/wiki/Jeju_Island "Jeju Island"), the southernmost territory of [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea").

##### Hawaii and Botswana

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Hawaii and Botswana")]

[Hawaii](/wiki/Hawaii "Hawaii") is antipodal to parts of [Botswana](/wiki/Botswana "Botswana"). The [Big Island](/wiki/Hawaii_(island) "Hawaii (island)") of [Hawaii](/wiki/Hawaii "Hawaii") is antipodal to the [Okavango Delta](/wiki/Okavango_Delta "Okavango Delta") in Botswana, with the island's largest city, [Hilo](/wiki/Hilo "Hilo"), antipodal to [Nxai Pan National Park](/wiki/Nxai_Pan_National_Park "Nxai Pan National Park").

##### Kerguelen Islands and North America

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: Kerguelen Islands and North America")]

The desolate [French](/wiki/France "France") territory of the [Kerguelen Islands](/wiki/Kerguelen_Islands "Kerguelen Islands") are antipodal to an area of sparsely inhabited prairie between [Cypress County](/wiki/Cypress_County "Cypress County"), [Alberta](/wiki/Alberta "Alberta"), and the [Rural Municipality of Reno](/wiki/Rural_Municipality_of_Reno_No._51 "Rural Municipality of Reno No. 51"), [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"); and [Liberty County](/wiki/Liberty_County,_Montana "Liberty County, Montana"), [Montana](/wiki/Montana "Montana"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). The only permanent settlement on Kerguelen Island, the research station [Port-aux-Français](/wiki/Port-aux-Fran%C3%A7ais "Port-aux-Français"), is antipodal to fields 10 km (6.2 mi) northeast of [Senate, Saskatchewan](/wiki/Senate,_Saskatchewan "Senate, Saskatchewan"). Other Canadian towns in Saskatchewan with antipodes on Kerguelen Island include: [Consul](/wiki/Consul,_Saskatchewan "Consul, Saskatchewan"), [Nashlyn](/wiki/Nashlyn "Nashlyn") and [Govenlock](/wiki/Govenlock "Govenlock") in the vicinity of Senate, and in Alberta [Eagle Butte](/wiki/Eagle_Butte,_Alberta "Eagle Butte, Alberta"), [Elkwater](/wiki/Elkwater,_Alberta "Elkwater, Alberta") and [Manyberries](/wiki/Manyberries "Manyberries") as well as the [Red Coat Trail](/wiki/Red_Coat_Trail "Red Coat Trail") between [Orion, Alberta](/wiki/Orion,_Alberta "Orion, Alberta") and [Etzikom](/wiki/Etzikom "Etzikom"). The communities of [Goldstone](/w/index.php?title=Goldstone,_Montana&action=edit&redlink=1 "Goldstone, Montana (page does not exist)"), Fox Crossing, and [Sage Creek Colony](/wiki/Sage_Creek_Colony "Sage Creek Colony") in [Montana](/wiki/Montana "Montana") have antipodes on Kerguelen Island.

##### St. Paul and Amsterdam Islands and Colorado

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=23 "Edit section: St. Paul and Amsterdam Islands and Colorado")]

[St. Paul Island](/wiki/%C3%8Ele_Saint-Paul "Île Saint-Paul") and [Amsterdam Island](/wiki/%C3%8Ele_Amsterdam "Île Amsterdam") are antipodal to sparsely populated parts of Eastern [Colorado](/wiki/Colorado "Colorado"), [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States"). They are situated approximately 10.2 km (6.3 mi) south-south-east of [Firstview](/wiki/Firstview,_Colorado "Firstview, Colorado") and 30.5 km (19.0 mi) south-south-west of [Granada, Colorado](/wiki/Granada,_Colorado "Granada, Colorado"), respectively. Together with the northern part of [Liberty County](/wiki/Liberty_County,_Montana "Liberty County, Montana"), [Montana](/wiki/Montana "Montana"), they are the only three areas of the [contiguous United States](/wiki/Contiguous_United_States "Contiguous United States") with antipodes on land.

##### Antarctica and North America

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: Antarctica and North America")]

The north-eastern coast of [Alaska](/wiki/Alaska "Alaska") from [Utqiaġvik](/wiki/Utqia%C4%A1vik "Utqiaġvik") (formerly Barrow) over [Prudhoe Bay](/wiki/Prudhoe_Bay "Prudhoe Bay") to the Canadian border, and the coasts of the Canadian territories of [Yukon](/wiki/Yukon "Yukon"), [Northwest Territories](/wiki/Northwest_Territories "Northwest Territories"), and [Nunavut](/wiki/Nunavut "Nunavut"), are antipodal to [Antarctica](/wiki/Antarctica "Antarctica").

##### Heard and McDonald Islands and Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=25 "Edit section: Heard and McDonald Islands and Canada")]

The [Heard Island and McDonald Islands](/wiki/Heard_Island_and_McDonald_Islands "Heard Island and McDonald Islands"), an uninhabited Australian territory, is antipodal to an area in central [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan"), [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada"), including the towns of [Leask](/wiki/Leask,_Saskatchewan "Leask, Saskatchewan") and [Shellbrook](/wiki/Shellbrook,_Saskatchewan "Shellbrook, Saskatchewan").

##### Angola and Johnston Atoll

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=26 "Edit section: Angola and Johnston Atoll")]

[Tigres Island](/wiki/Tigres_Island "Tigres Island"), the largest uninhabited island of [Angola](/wiki/Angola "Angola"), is approximately antipodal to [Johnston Atoll](/wiki/Johnston_atoll "Johnston atoll"), which is the third largest uninhabited island of the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States").

##### Easter Island and India

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=27 "Edit section: Easter Island and India")]

[Easter Island](/wiki/Easter_Island "Easter Island") is antipodal to an area close to [Desert National Park](/wiki/Desert_National_Park "Desert National Park"), 35 km (22 mi) from [Jaisalmer](/wiki/Jaisalmer "Jaisalmer"), [India](/wiki/India "India"). The only town on Easter Island, [Hanga Roa](/wiki/Hanga_Roa "Hanga Roa"), is antipodal to the village of Serawa 46 km (29 mi) northeast of Jaisalmer. Serawa is the only village in India to be antipodal to a human settlement. Its neighbouring villages Mokla and the northern part of Bhadasar also have antipodes on Easter Island. The small, rocky, uninhabited island of [Sala y Gómez](/wiki/Isla_Salas_y_Gomez "Isla Salas y Gomez"), 391 km (243 mi) east-northeast of Easter Island, is antipodal to an area in the city of [Ajmer](/wiki/Ajmer "Ajmer"), India, just east of [Ana Sagar Lake](/wiki/Ana_Sagar_Lake "Ana Sagar Lake"). The rest of India has antipodes in the sea.

##### Kiribati and the Democratic Republic of the Congo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=28 "Edit section: Kiribati and the Democratic Republic of the Congo")]

[Kiritimati](/wiki/Kiritimati "Kiritimati"), the largest island of [Kiribati](/wiki/Kiribati "Kiribati") and the largest coral atoll in the world, is antipodal to [Salonga National Park](/wiki/Salonga_National_Park "Salonga National Park"), which is the largest national park of the [Democratic Republic of the Congo](/wiki/Democratic_Republic_of_the_Congo "Democratic Republic of the Congo") and the largest tropical rainforest reserve in Africa.

##### Portugal and New Zealand

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=29 "Edit section: Portugal and New Zealand")]

[Serra da Estrela Natural Park](/wiki/Serra_da_Estrela_Natural_Park "Serra da Estrela Natural Park"), the largest natural park of [Portugal](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal"), is antipodal to [Kahurangi National Park](/wiki/Kahurangi_National_Park "Kahurangi National Park"), the second largest national park of [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand").

##### South Georgia Island and Russia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=30 "Edit section: South Georgia Island and Russia")]

[South Georgia Island](/wiki/South_Georgia "South Georgia") (part of the [British Overseas Territory](/wiki/British_Overseas_Territories "British Overseas Territories") of [South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands](/wiki/South_Georgia_and_the_South_Sandwich_Islands "South Georgia and the South Sandwich Islands")) is antipodal to the northernmost part of [Sakhalin Island](/wiki/Sakhalin_Island "Sakhalin Island"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia").

##### Lake Baikal and the Strait of Magellan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=31 "Edit section: Lake Baikal and the Strait of Magellan")]

[Lake Baikal](/wiki/Lake_Baikal "Lake Baikal"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia") is partially antipodal to the [Strait of Magellan](/wiki/Strait_of_Magellan "Strait of Magellan").

##### Bellinghausen Station, Antarctica and Siberia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=32 "Edit section: Bellinghausen Station, Antarctica and Siberia")]

The Russian Antarctic research base [Bellingshausen Station](/wiki/Bellingshausen_Station "Bellingshausen Station") is antipodal to a land location in [Siberia](/wiki/Siberia "Siberia"), [Russia](/wiki/Russia "Russia").

##### Australia and Bermuda

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=33 "Edit section: Australia and Bermuda")]

[Rottnest Island](/wiki/Rottnest_Island "Rottnest Island"), off the coast of [Western Australia](/wiki/Western_Australia "Western Australia"), is approximately antipodal to the [British Overseas Territory](/wiki/British_Overseas_Territories "British Overseas Territories") of [Bermuda](/wiki/Bermuda "Bermuda").

##### Cocos (Keeling) Islands and Nicaragua

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=34 "Edit section: Cocos (Keeling) Islands and Nicaragua")]

[Cocos (Keeling) Islands](/wiki/Cocos_(Keeling)_Islands "Cocos (Keeling) Islands"), an Australian external territory in the Indian Ocean, is almost antipodal to [Nicaragua](/wiki/Nicaragua "Nicaragua")'s [Corn Islands](/wiki/Corn_Islands "Corn Islands").

##### Azores and Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=35 "Edit section: Azores and Australia")]

[Flores Island](/wiki/Flores_Island_(Azores) "Flores Island (Azores)"), the westernmost island of the [Azores](/wiki/Azores "Azores"), a [Portuguese](/wiki/Portugal "Portugal") archipelago in the [Atlantic Ocean](/wiki/Atlantic_Ocean "Atlantic Ocean"), is nearly antipodal to [Flinders Island](/wiki/Flinders_Island "Flinders Island") between [Tasmania](/wiki/Tasmania "Tasmania") and the Australian mainland.

##### Point Nemo and Kazakhstan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=36 "Edit section: Point Nemo and Kazakhstan")]

[Point Nemo](/wiki/Pole_of_inaccessibility#Oceanic_pole_of_inaccessibility "Pole of inaccessibility"), the point in the Southern Pacific Ocean most distant from any other land, is precisely opposite a desolate piece of desert in western [Kazakhstan](/wiki/Kazakhstan "Kazakhstan").

##### 0°N 0°E and 0°N 180°E

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=37 "Edit section: 0°N 0°E and 0°N 180°E")]

[Null Island](/wiki/Null_Island "Null Island"), [0°N 0°E﻿ / ﻿0°N 0°E﻿ / 0; 0](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Antipodes&params=0_N_0_E_), at the intersection of the [prime meridian](/wiki/Prime_meridian "Prime meridian") and the [equator](/wiki/Equator "Equator"), has its antipodes at [0°N 180°E﻿ / ﻿0°N 180°E﻿ / 0; 180](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Antipodes&params=0_N_180_E_), at the intersection of the [antimeridian](/wiki/Antimeridian "Antimeridian") and the [equator](/wiki/Equator "Equator"). This point lies northeast of [Nikunau](/wiki/Nikunau "Nikunau") in the [Gilbert Islands](/wiki/Gilbert_Islands "Gilbert Islands") and southwest of [Baker Island](/wiki/Baker_Island "Baker Island"), a [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") territory.

##### Pacific Ocean

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=38 "Edit section: Pacific Ocean")]

As can be seen on the purple/blue map, the [Pacific Ocean](/wiki/Pacific_Ocean "Pacific Ocean") is so large that it stretches halfway around the world; parts of the Pacific off the coast of [Peru](/wiki/Peru "Peru") are antipodal to parts of the same ocean off the coast of [Southeast Asia](/wiki/Southeast_Asia "Southeast Asia"). For example, the island of [Ko Chang](/wiki/Ko_Chang "Ko Chang")—which is the second or third largest island in [Thailand](/wiki/Thailand "Thailand")—is nearly antipodal to [San Lorenzo Island](/wiki/San_Lorenzo_Island,_Peru "San Lorenzo Island, Peru"), which is the largest island of [Peru](/wiki/Peru "Peru").

##### Antipodes Islands and France

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=39 "Edit section: Antipodes Islands and France")]

The antipodes of the [Antipodes Islands](/wiki/Antipodes_Islands "Antipodes Islands"), [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand"), considered by early European explorers to be antipodal to the United Kingdom, are the town of [Barfleur](/wiki/Barfleur "Barfleur") on [France's](/wiki/France "France") [Cotentin Peninsula](/wiki/Cotentin_Peninsula "Cotentin Peninsula").

##### Mecca and French Polynesia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=40 "Edit section: Mecca and French Polynesia")]

The remote Pacific [atoll](/wiki/Atoll "Atoll") of [Tematagi](/wiki/Tematagi "Tematagi"), [French Polynesia](/wiki/French_Polynesia "French Polynesia") is antipodal to the Islamic holy city of [Mecca](/wiki/Mecca "Mecca"), [Saudi Arabia](/wiki/Saudi_Arabia "Saudi Arabia"), meaning the direction of [Muslim](/wiki/Muslims "Muslims") prayer would vary widely from that of surrounding islands.

##### Angkor Wat and Machu Picchu

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=41 "Edit section: Angkor Wat and Machu Picchu")]

[Angkor Wat](/wiki/Angkor_Wat "Angkor Wat"), [Cambodia](/wiki/Cambodia "Cambodia") is roughly antipodal to [Machu Picchu](/wiki/Machu_Picchu "Machu Picchu"), Peru.

## Antipodes beyond Earth

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=43 "Edit section: Antipodes beyond Earth")]

In a number of cases on extraterrestrial bodies in the Solar System, unusual geologic features (e.g., jumbled terrain or unique volcanic constructs) are located antipodal to major impact basins. It has been hypothesized that this results from focusing of some of the seismic waves ([p-waves](/wiki/P-wave "P-wave") and [surface waves](/wiki/Surface_wave#Mechanical_waves "Surface wave")) produced by an impact at its antipode.[[36]](#cite_note-Schultz1975-38)

* [Caloris Basin](/wiki/Caloris_Basin "Caloris Basin") – "Weird Terrain" ([Mercury](/wiki/Mercury_(planet) "Mercury (planet)"))[[36]](#cite_note-Schultz1975-38)
* [Mare Orientale](/wiki/Mare_Orientale "Mare Orientale") – [Mare Marginis](/wiki/Mare_Marginis "Mare Marginis") (The [Moon](/wiki/Moon "Moon"))[[36]](#cite_note-Schultz1975-38)
* [Mare Imbrium](/wiki/Mare_Imbrium "Mare Imbrium") – [Mare Ingenii](/wiki/Mare_Ingenii "Mare Ingenii") (The [Moon](/wiki/Moon "Moon"))[[36]](#cite_note-Schultz1975-38)
* [Hellas Planitia](/wiki/Hellas_Planitia "Hellas Planitia") – [Alba Mons](/wiki/Alba_Mons "Alba Mons") ([Mars](/wiki/Mars "Mars"))[[37]](#cite_note-Peterson1978-39)[[38]](#cite_note-Williams1991-40)[[39]](#cite_note-Williams1994-41)
* [Isidis Planitia](/wiki/Isidis_Planitia "Isidis Planitia") – [Noctis Labyrinthus](/wiki/Noctis_Labyrinthus "Noctis Labyrinthus") ([Mars](/wiki/Mars "Mars"))[[37]](#cite_note-Peterson1978-39)[[38]](#cite_note-Williams1991-40)[[39]](#cite_note-Williams1994-41)
* [Kerwan](/wiki/Kerwan_(crater) "Kerwan (crater)") – [Ahuna Mons](/wiki/Ahuna_Mons "Ahuna Mons") ([Ceres](/wiki/Ceres_(dwarf_planet) "Ceres (dwarf planet)"))[[40]](#cite_note-Ruesch2016-42)

## In popular culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Antipodes&action=edit&section=44 "Edit section: In popular culture")]

* In the [Shakespeare](/wiki/William_Shakespeare "William Shakespeare") comedy *[Much Ado About Nothing](/wiki/Much_Ado_About_Nothing "Much Ado About Nothing")*, Benedick offers to travel from [Messina](/wiki/Messina "Messina") to the Antipodes in an apparent attempt to avoid the company of [Beatrice](/wiki/Beatrice_(Much_Ado_About_Nothing) "Beatrice (Much Ado About Nothing)").[[41]](#cite_note-43)
* On the TV show *[Angel](/wiki/Angel_(1999_TV_series) "Angel (1999 TV series)")*, the Deeper Well is a hole that goes through the world, with its entrance in the [Cotswolds](/wiki/Cotswolds "Cotswolds") in England and its antipode in New Zealand.
* At the [closing ceremonies of the Rio 2016 Olympics](/wiki/2016_Summer_Olympics_closing_ceremony#Warming_up!_Tokyo_2020 "2016 Summer Olympics closing ceremony"), antipodes were used as a tool to invite viewers to the [Tokyo 2020 Olympics](/wiki/2020_Olympics "2020 Olympics"), including an image of the video game character [Mario](/wiki/Mario "Mario") using his pipes to travel between Tokyo and Rio, arriving at the closing ceremonies.[[42]](#cite_note-44)
* In the 2012 film *[Total Recall](/wiki/Total_Recall_(2012_film) "Total Recall (2012 film)")*, a [gravity train](/wiki/Gravity_train "Gravity train") called "The Fall" goes through the center of the Earth to allow people to commute between Western Europe and Australia.[[43]](#cite_note-45)[[44]](#cite_note-46)
* In 2006, [Ze Frank](/wiki/Ze_Frank "Ze Frank") challenged viewers of his daily webcast *[the show with zefrank](/wiki/The_show_with_zefrank "The show with zefrank")* to create an "Earth sandwich" by simultaneously placing two pieces of [bread](/wiki/Bread "Bread") at antipodal points on the Earth's surface. The challenge was successfully completed by viewers in [Spain](/wiki/Spain "Spain") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand").[[45]](#cite_note-47)
* The song "[Ana Ng](/wiki/Ana_Ng "Ana Ng")" by alternative rock band [They Might Be Giants](/wiki/They_Might_Be_Giants "They Might Be Giants") is about someone who believes that their soulmate lives antipodal to them. [John Linnell](/wiki/John_Linnell "John Linnell"), the singer and songwriter, has since joked that because the name [Ng](/wiki/Ng_(name) "Ng (name)") is Vietnamese and [Peru](/wiki/Peru "Peru") is the antipode of [Vietnam](/wiki/Vietnam "Vietnam"), "…the song, presumably, is about somebody in Peru, writing about somebody in Vietnam. But I didn't know that when I wrote it."[[46]](#cite_note-48)
